name: The Gold Rush Store #1 Gold Jewelry buyer in Nashville Tn
description_short: Smyrna, TN precious metals and jewelry buyer serving the Nashville area. Buys and sells gold, silver, platinum, diamonds, coins, and bullion by appointment.
description_long:
The Gold Rush Store is a precious metals and jewelry buying and selling business located in Smyrna, Tennessee, serving the Nashville metro and surrounding areas. The company positions itself as the number one buyer of gold, silver, bullion, coins, and jewelry in the Nashville region. They operate out of a single Smyrna location and are available Monday through Saturday by appointment only, suggesting a personalized, one-on-one service model rather than a traditional walk-in retail environment.

The store buys and sells a wide variety of precious metals and related items. On the buying side, they accept gold in nearly all forms — including dental gold, body jewelry, broken chains, vintage charms, and gold coins — as well as silver items such as sterling flatware and rings, platinum, diamonds, and bullion. They also sell items, positioning themselves as a source for fine jewelry and collectibles for shoppers in the Smyrna community.

What distinguishes The Gold Rush Store from general pawn shops is its narrow focus on precious metals and gemstones rather than electronics, tools, or general merchandise. The appointment-only format implies customers receive dedicated attention and expert evaluation rather than a rushed counter experience. Their marketing emphasizes expert advice and community familiarity, framing the transaction as collaborative rather than adversarial — a pitch aimed at first-time sellers who may be nervous about getting a fair price.

For consumers, The Gold Rush Store is a reasonable option if you have unwanted or broken gold, silver, or diamond jewelry and want to convert it to cash. However, the website provides no pricing benchmarks, no fee disclosures, and no information about how valuations are conducted. As with any precious metals buyer, sellers should get competing quotes before agreeing to a sale. The absence of pawn loan services means this is a straight buy-sell operation — there is no option to reclaim items after selling.
pros: Accepts a wide range of gold forms including dental gold, body jewelry, and broken or vintage pieces
Also buys coins and bullion, making it useful for collectors liquidating holdings
Appointment-only model suggests dedicated, personalized service per customer
Claims top-dollar pricing as a core value proposition
Buys and sells, so customers can shop for jewelry as well as sell
Monday–Saturday availability offers flexibility for working customers
Focused exclusively on precious metals and gems — specialized expertise in one niche
cons: Appointment-only — no walk-in service, requiring advance scheduling
Single Smyrna, TN location limits accessibility for Nashville proper and outlying areas
No loan or pawn option — items sold cannot be reclaimed later
Website contains no pricing information, valuation methodology, or fee disclosures
No customer reviews, ratings, or third-party verification cited on the website
best_for: Homeowners or heirs looking to sell inherited gold jewelry, coins, or silverware
People with broken, outdated, or unwanted gold and silver items seeking cash
Coin and bullion holders looking to liquidate precious metal holdings locally
Shoppers seeking secondhand fine jewelry at potentially below-retail prices
diagnosis: The Gold Rush Store is best suited for individuals in the Nashville–Smyrna area who want to sell precious metals, jewelry, coins, or bullion and prefer a focused, appointment-based experience over a traditional pawn shop. The main caveat is that pricing and valuation processes are entirely opaque on the website, so sellers should treat this as one of multiple quotes to solicit rather than an automatic best offer.
